knowledge.html:
I. PRESENT TENSES

1. Hiện tại đơn (Simple Present)

Công thức/Từ nhận biếtCách dùng
Động từ TO BE:
(+): S + am/is/are...
Động từ thường:
(+): S + Vs/es/V(nguyên mẫu)
(-): S + do/does + not + V(bare)
(?): Do/does + S + V(bare)?
Từ nhận biết:
- seldom/ rarely/ hardly
- sometimes/ occasionally
- often/ usually/ frequently
- always/ constantly/ ever/ never/ every		- diễn tả hành động thường xuyên xảy ra.
- diễn tả thói quen.
- diễn tả thời gian biểu, lịch trình, thông báo.
- diễn tả sự thật, chân lí.
- diễn tả nghề nghiệp, sở thích, nguồn gốc, bình phẩm.

2. Hiện tại tiếp diễn (Present Continuous)

Công thức/Từ nhận biếtCách dùng
(+): S + am/is/are + V-ing
(-): S + am/is/are + not + V-ing
(?): Am/Is/Are + S + V-ing?
Từ nhận biết:
- now, at the moment, at present, right now, look, hear (!)		- diễn tả hành động đang xảy ra tại thời điểm nói.
- diễn tả hành động sẽ xảy ra trong tương lai (có kế hoạch từ trước).
- diễn tả sự thay đổi của thói quen.
- diễn tả sự cả thán, phàn nàn.

3. Hiện tại hoàn thành (Present Perfect)

Công thức/Từ nhận biếtCách dùng
(+): S + have/has + V(pp)
(Have: I/số nhiều - Has: số ít)
(-): S + have/has + not + V(pp)
(?): Have/Has + S + V(pp)?
Từ nhận biết:
- for, since, ever, never, so far, recently, lately, before (dùng cuối câu), yet, just, already, up to now, up to present, until now		- diễn tả hành động xảy ra trong quá khứ nhưng không rõ thời gian.
- diễn tả hành động lặp đi lặp lại nhiều lần trong quá khứ.
- diễn tả hành động xảy ra trong quá khứ nhưng để lại dấu hiệu hoặc hậu quả ở hiện tại.
- diễn tả những trải nghiệm.
- diễn tả những hành động xảy ra trong quá khứ nhưng kéo dài tới hiện tại và vẫn còn có khả năng sẽ tiếp diễn trong tương lai.

II. PAST TENSES

1. Quá khứ đơn (Simple Past)

Công thức/Từ nhận biếtCách dùng
Động từ TO BE:
(+): S + was/were
Động từ thường:
(+): S + V3/ed
(-): S + did + not + V(bare)
(?): Did + S + V(bare)?
Từ nhận biết:
- ago, last, yesterday, in + một mốc thời gian trong quá khứ (in 2000...)		- diễn tả hành động đã xảy ra và đã chấm dứt trong quá khứ, không còn liên quan tới hiện tại.
- diễn tả hành động xảy ra nối tiếp nhau trong quá khứ.
- diễn tả hồi ức, kỉ niệm.

2. Quá khứ tiếp diễn (Past Continuous)

Công thức/Từ nhận biếtCách dùng
(+): S + was/were + V-ing
(-): S + was/were + not + V-ing
(?): Was/Were + S + V-ing?
Từ nhận biết:
- giờ + trạng từ quá khứ (at 3 pm yesterday...)
- at this/that time + trạng từ quá khứ (at this time last week...)		- diễn tả hành động đang xảy ra tại một thời điểm xác định trong quá khứ.
- diễn tả hành động đang xảy ra thì có hành động khác xen vào (hành động nào xảy ra trước chia thì quá khứ tiếp diễn, hành động nào xảy ra sau chia thì quá khứ đơn).

3. Quá khứ hoàn thành (Past Perfect)

Công thức/Từ nhận biếtCách dùng
(+): S + had + V(pp)
(-): S + had + not + V(pp)
(?): Had + S + V(pp)?
Từ nhận biết:
- before/by the time (trước chia quá khứ hoàn thành, sau chia quá khứ đơn).
- after (trước chia quá khứ đơn, sau chia quá khứ hoàn thành).		- diễn tả những hành động xảy ra và hoàn thành trước hành động khác trong quá khứ.

III. FUTURE TENSES

1. Tương lai đơn (Simple Future)

Công thức/Từ nhận biếtCách dùng
(+): S + will + V(bare)
(-): S + will + not + V(bare)
(?): Will + S + V(bare)?
Từ nhận biết:
- tomorrow, next, soon, in + một khoảng thời gian (in an hour...)		- diễn tả những hành động sẽ xảy ra trong tương lai.
- diễn tả những dự đoán.
- diễn tả lời hứa.

2. Tương lai tiếp diễn (Future Continuous)

Công thức/Từ nhận biếtCách dùng
(+): S + will + be + V-ing
(-): S + will + not + be + V-ing
(?): Will + S + be + V-ing?
Từ nhận biết:
- giờ + trạng từ tương lai (at 3 pm tomorrow...)
- at this/that time + trạng từ tương lai (at this time next week...)		- diễn tả hành động đang diễn ra vào một thời điểm cụ thể trong tương lai.
- diễn tả hành động sẽ đang xảy ra trong tương lai thì có hành động khác xen vào.

3. Tương lai hoàn thành (Future Perfect)

Công thứcCách dùng
(+): S + will + have + V(pp)
(-): S + will + not + have + V(pp)
(?): Will + S + have + V(pp)?
- diễn tả hành động sẽ được hoàn thành trước khi một hành động khác xảy đến.

IV. KIẾN THỨC BỔ TRỢ

It is + thời gian + since + S + last + Vpast
= The last time S + Vpast + was + thời gian (ago)
= S + last + Vpast + thời gian (ago)
= S + haven't/hasn't + PII + for/since + thời gian
Examples: It is 4 years since I last went to Japan.
= The last time I went to Japan was 4 years ago.
= I last went to Japan 4 years ago.
= I haven't gone to Japan for 4 years.
S + started/began + Ving/to V + thời gian (ago)
= S + have/has + PII + for/since + thời gian
Examples: David started working here last year.
= David has worked here since last year.
S + have/has never + PII + before
= S + haven't/hasn't + PII + before
= This is the first time + S + have/has + PII
Examples: We have never played golf before.
= We haven't played golf before.
= This is the first time we have played golf.
This/S + so sánh nhất + N + S + have/has ever + PII
= S + have/has never + PII + such (a/an) + N(s) + like this(as) + S + (before)
Examples: This is the most interesting novel I have ever read.
= I have never read such an interesting novel like this before.
When + did + S + V?
= How long is it since + S + Vpast?
Examples: When did you buy this car?
= How long is it since you bought this car?

exercises.question: He has been selling motorbikes _______.
exercises.explanation: Present perfect continuous uses 'for' + duration. 'For tên years' = trong 10 năm.

exercises.question: Christopher Columbus _______ American more than 500 years ago.
exercises.explanation: '500 years ago' signals simple past tense.

exercises.question: Since _______, I have heard nothing from him.
exercises.explanation: 'Since' + simple past clause pairs with present perfect in the main clause.

exercises.question: The man got out of the car, _______ round to the back and opened the book.
exercises.explanation: Three past actions in sequence all use simple past: got out, walked, opened.

exercises.question: Her father _______ when she was a small girl.
exercises.explanation: 'When she was a small girl' sets a past time frame, so use simple past.

exercises.question: Her husband _______ unemployed since they _______ to the countryside.
exercises.explanation: 'Since' + simple past (moved) pairs with present perfect (has been) in the main clause.

exercises.question: He fell down when he _______ towards the church.
exercises.explanation: An ongoing action (past continuous) was interrupted by another action (fell down - past simple).

exercises.question: While they were laying the table, he _______ to the radio.
exercises.explanation: Two simultaneous ongoing past actions both use past continuous.

exercises.question: Henry _______ into the restaurant when the writer was having dinner.
exercises.explanation: A short action (went) interrupts an ongoing action (was having). The short action uses simple past.

exercises.question: By the age of 25, he _______ two famous novels.
exercises.explanation: 'By the age of 25' refers to a point in the past before which the action was completed - use past perfect.

exercises.question: He always _______ for a walk in the evening.
exercises.explanation: 'Always' signals a habitual action - use simple present. 'He' takes 'goes'.

exercises.question: I'll come and see you before I _______ for the States.
exercises.explanation: After 'before' in a future context, use simple present (not will).

exercises.question: He will take the dog out for a walk as soon as he _______ dinner.
exercises.explanation: After 'as soon as' in future context, use simple present. 'He' takes 'finishes'.

exercises.question: Her brother _______ in Canada at present.
exercises.explanation: 'At present' signals an action happening now - use present continuous.

exercises.question: Tom and Mary _______ for Vietnam tomorrow.
exercises.explanation: Present continuous for a planned future arrangement. 'Tomorrow' confirms future plan.

exercises.question: We _______ Dorothy since last Saturday.
exercises.explanation: 'Since last Saturday' signals present perfect: haven't seen.

exercises.question: It is blowing so hard. We _______ such a terrible storm before.
exercises.explanation: Experience up to now uses present perfect: 'have never known'.

exercises.question: I have never played badminton before. This is the first time I _______ to play.
exercises.explanation: 'This is the first time' requires present perfect: 'have tried'.

exercises.question: Ask her to come and see me when she _______ her work.
exercises.explanation: After 'when' in future context, present perfect can show completed action before the main action.

exercises.question: Our industrial output _______ from $2 million in 2002 to $4 million this year.
exercises.explanation: The period extends to 'this year' (present), so use present perfect: 'has risen'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Daisy will sitting on the plane at 5 a.m tomorrow.
exercises.correctSentence: Daisy will be sitting on the plane at 5 a.m tomorrow.
exercises.explanation: Future continuous: will + be + V-ing. 'At 5 a.m tomorrow' is a specific future time.

exercises.wrongSentence: What will you doing at 11 p.m tonight?
exercises.correctSentence: What will you be doing at 11 p.m tonight?
exercises.explanation: Future continuous question: Will + S + be + V-ing?

exercises.wrongSentence: By this time tomorrow, they will visit Paris with their friends.
exercises.correctSentence: By this time tomorrow, they will have visited Paris with their friends.
exercises.explanation: 'By this time tomorrow' signals future perfect: will + have + V(pp).

exercises.wrongSentence: By 2030, the number of schools in our city will have double.
exercises.correctSentence: By 2030, the number of schools in our city will have doubled.
exercises.explanation: Future perfect: will + have + past participle. 'Double' -> 'doubled'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Some people are believing that there is life on the Moon.
exercises.correctSentence: Some people believe that there is life on the Moon.
exercises.explanation: 'Believe' is a stative verb and cannot be used in continuous form.

exercises.wrongSentence: We will have walked to the bus stop at this time next morning.
exercises.correctSentence: We will be walking to the bus stop at this time next morning.
exercises.explanation: 'At this time next morning' = a specific moment in the future -> future continuous.

exercises.wrongSentence: My father and I go skiing almost every day last winter.
exercises.correctSentence: My father and I went skiing almost every day last winter.
exercises.explanation: 'Last winter' is a past time marker, so use simple past: 'went'.

exercises.wrongSentence: What time has the train to Hai Phong leave?
exercises.correctSentence: What time does the train to Hai Phong leave?
exercises.explanation: Timetables/schedules use simple present with auxiliary 'does'.

exercises.wrongSentence: I have been waiting for you since two hours.
exercises.correctSentence: I have been waiting for you for two hours.
exercises.explanation: Use 'for' with a duration (two hours). 'Since' is for a specific point in time (since 2 ở'clock).

exercises.wrongSentence: Look! Her eyes are red. She had been crying.
exercises.correctSentence: Look! Her eyes are red. She has been crying.
exercises.explanation: 'Look!' signals present context. Use present perfect continuous, not past perfect continuous.

exercises.original: It is a long time since we last met.
exercises.answer: We haven't met for a long time.
acceptedAnswers: We haven't met for a long time.
We haven't met for a long time
exercises.explanation: 'It is + time + since + S + last + Vpast' = 'S + haven't + PII + for + time'.

exercises.original: When did you have this computer?
exercises.answer: How long have you had this computer?
acceptedAnswers: How long have you had this computer?
How long have you had this computer
exercises.explanation: 'When did + S + V?' = 'How long + have/has + S + PII?'

exercises.original: My nephew James has never drunk beer before.
exercises.answer: This is the first time my nephew James has drunk beer.
acceptedAnswers: This is the first time my nephew James has drunk beer.
This is the first time my nephew James has drunk beer
exercises.explanation: 'S + have/has never + PII + before' = 'This is the first time + S + have/has + PII'. Note: 'drunk' is the past participle of 'drink' (drink-drank-drunk).

exercises.original: This is the first time he visited Ha Long Bay.
exercises.answer: He has never visited Ha Long Bay before.
acceptedAnswers: He has never visited Ha Long Bay before.
He has never visited Ha Long Bay before
exercises.explanation: 'This is the first time + S + V' = 'S + have/has never + PII + before'.

exercises.original: She started working here last year.
exercises.answer: She has worked here since last year.
acceptedAnswers: She has worked here since last year.
She has worked here since last year
exercises.explanation: 'S + started + Ving + time' = 'S + have/has + PII + since + time'.

exercises.original: We began eating lunch when it started to rain.
exercises.answer: We have been eating lunch since it started to rain.
acceptedAnswers: We have been eating lunch since it started to rain.
We have been eating lunch since it started to rain
We have been eating lunch since it started raining.
We have been eating lunch since it started raining
exercises.explanation: 'S + began + Ving + when + past' = 'S + have/has been + Ving + since + past clause'. Use present perfect continuous because the action started and is still ongoing.

exercises.original: I last had my hair cut when she left me.
exercises.answer: I haven't had my hair cut since she left me.
acceptedAnswers: I haven't had my hair cut since she left me.
I haven't had my hair cut since she left me
exercises.explanation: 'S + last + Vpast + when + clause' = 'S + haven't/hasn't + PII + since + clause'.

exercises.original: The last time he met me was 5 months ago.
exercises.answer: He hasn't met me for 5 months.
acceptedAnswers: He hasn't met me for 5 months.
He hasn't met me for 5 months
He hasn't met me for five months.
He hasn't met me for five months
exercises.explanation: 'The last time + S + Vpast + was + time ago' = 'S + haven't/hasn't + PII + for + time'.

exercises.original: When did they start opening this shopping center?
exercises.answer: How long have they been opening this shopping center?
acceptedAnswers: How long have they been opening this shopping center?
How long have they been opening this shopping center
exercises.explanation: 'When did + S + start + Ving?' = 'How long + have/has + S + been + Ving?' Here 'opening' is kept from the original sentence's verb form.

exercises.original: I haven't been to the zoo for over a year.
exercises.answer: The last time I went to the zoo was over a year ago.
acceptedAnswers: The last time I went to the zoo was over a year ago.
The last time I went to the zoo was over a year ago
exercises.explanation: 'S + haven't + PII + for + time' = 'The last time + S + Vpast + was + time + ago'.
